name: Ben Bernie
description: American jazz violinist, bandleader, radio personality (1891–1943)
abstract: Benjamin Anzelevitz (May 30, 1891 – October 20, 1943), known professionally as Ben Bernie, was an American jazz violinist, bandleader, and radio personality, often introduced as "The Old Maestro". He was noted for his showmanship and memorable bits of snappy dialogue, being part of the first generation of "stars" of American popular musi…

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Barnet has a small acting part. Which consisted of standing in front of a band and waving a baton and trying to look like the band was really playing.

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: I turned in a report that Ben Bernie and his orchestra were opening at McGinnis of Sheepshead Bay... That shows how much I knew.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Ben Bernie and Tim. [INTERVIEWEE] And the Kansas City Nighthawks went to Chicago, and Coon-Sanders.

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: I bought Paul Whiteman's records, Ben Bernie's records, and then I got to know all those fellows later on

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: I did some things with Ben Bernie... when he went in the College Inn, I went in with him

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: I remember, Dix Debill was the conductor for Ben Bernie’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995
